title: ageratum conyzoides subsp. houstonianum
intro: Ageratum conyzoides subsp. houstonianum, also known as Mexican bluebell or Houston's ageratum, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties that can be used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Ageratum conyzoides subsp. houstonianum include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
